01 — Analyse Exploratoire des Données (EDA) — version corrigée¶

Projet PFE : Système Intelligent de Gestion de l'Eau — Grand Agadir

⚠️ Correction clé : le pas de temps du dataset est de 15 minutes (96 points / jour), pas 1 heure. Tous les libellés, la saisonnalité et les analyses en tiennent compte (cycle journalier = lag 96).

Objectifs : charger/inspecter, distributions, patterns temporels (15 min, jour, semaine, saison), analyse par zone/quartier, météo, anomalies/fuites, décomposition, ACF/PACF, carte, split train/test.

0. Imports & Configuration¶

In [1]:
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.dates as mdates
import seaborn as sns

from statsmodels.tsa.seasonal import seasonal_decompose
from statsmodels.graphics.tsaplots import plot_acf, plot_pacf
from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import adfuller

sns.set_theme(style='whitegrid', palette='muted', font_scale=1.1)
plt.rcParams.update({'figure.dpi': 110, 'figure.figsize': (14, 5),
                     'axes.titlesize': 14, 'axes.labelsize': 12})

DATA_PATH = Path('../data/water_consumption_agadir_pfe.csv')
FIG_DIR   = Path('../reports/figures'); FIG_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

# Pas de temps réel du dataset
STEPS_PER_DAY  = 96   # 24 h * 4 (un point toutes les 15 min)
STEPS_PER_WEEK = STEPS_PER_DAY * 7
print('✅ Imports OK | pas de temps = 15 min →', STEPS_PER_DAY, 'points/jour')

✅ Imports OK | pas de temps = 15 min → 96 points/jour

1. Chargement & Inspection Initiale¶

In [2]:
df = pd.read_csv(DATA_PATH, parse_dates=['timestamp'])
df.columns = df.columns.str.strip().str.lower()
df['timestamp'] = pd.to_datetime(df['timestamp'])
df = df.sort_values(['meterid', 'timestamp']).reset_index(drop=True)

# Vérification du pas de temps réel
step = df.sort_values('timestamp').groupby('meterid')['timestamp'].diff().dropna().mode()[0]
print(f'📐 Shape : {df.shape}')
print(f'📅 Période : {df.timestamp.min()} → {df.timestamp.max()}')
print(f'⏱  Pas de temps détecté : {step}  (→ {int(pd.Timedelta("1D")/step)} points/jour)')
print(f'🔢 Compteurs : {df.meterid.nunique()} | Zones : {df.zone.nunique()}')
print(f'🗂  Colonnes ({len(df.columns)}) :', list(df.columns))

📐 Shape : (277644, 19)
📅 Période : 2025-02-01 00:00:00 → 2025-09-30 00:00:00
⏱  Pas de temps détecté : 0 days 00:15:00  (→ 96 points/jour)
🔢 Compteurs : 12 | Zones : 3
🗂  Colonnes (19) : ['timestamp', 'meterid', 'zone', 'quartier', 'latitude', 'longitude', 'consumptionliters', 'flowratelpm', 'pressurebar', 'temperaturec', 'rainfall', 'hour', 'dayofweek', 'month', 'isweekend', 'season', 'household_size', 'event_type', 'anomalylabel']

2. Distribution de la cible : consumptionliters (litres / 15 min)¶

In [6]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
axes[0].hist(df['consumptionliters'], bins=80, color='steelblue', edgecolor='white', alpha=0.85)
axes[0].set_title('Distribution complète'); axes[0].set_xlabel('Litres / 15 min')

p5, p95 = df['consumptionliters'].quantile([0.05, 0.95])
dz = df['consumptionliters'].clip(p5, p95)
axes[1].hist(dz, bins=60, color='teal', edgecolor='white', alpha=0.85)
axes[1].set_title('Zoom p5–p95'); axes[1].set_xlabel('Litres / 15 min')

zones = df['zone'].unique()
axes[2].boxplot([df.loc[df.zone==z,'consumptionliters'] for z in zones], labels=zones,
                patch_artist=True, showfliers=False,
                boxprops=dict(facecolor='lightsteelblue'),
                medianprops=dict(color='navy', linewidth=2))
axes[2].set_title('Par zone (litres / 15 min)')
plt.suptitle('Distribution de la consommation', fontweight='bold', y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig01_distribution_consommation.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()
print(f"Skewness : {df['consumptionliters'].skew():.2f} | Kurtosis : {df['consumptionliters'].kurtosis():.2f}")

Skewness : 5.55 | Kurtosis : 65.69

3. Série temporelle globale (consommation journalière agrégée)¶

In [7]:
daily = df.groupby(df['timestamp'].dt.date)['consumptionliters'].sum()
daily.index = pd.to_datetime(daily.index); daily = daily.sort_index()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 5))
ax.plot(daily.index, daily.values/1000, color='steelblue', lw=1.2)
ax.fill_between(daily.index, daily.values/1000, alpha=0.15, color='steelblue')
ax.set_title('Consommation journalière totale — toutes zones', fontweight='bold')
ax.set_ylabel('Milliers de litres / jour')
ax.xaxis.set_major_formatter(mdates.DateFormatter('%b %Y'))
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig02_serie_journaliere.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()

4. Patterns cycliques — par 15 min (heure), jour de semaine, mois¶

In [8]:
day_labels   = ['Lun','Mar','Mer','Jeu','Ven','Sam','Dim']
month_labels = {2:'Fév',3:'Mar',4:'Avr',5:'Mai',6:'Jun',7:'Jul',8:'Aoû',9:'Sep'}

hourly  = df.groupby('hour')['consumptionliters'].mean()
weekday = df.groupby('dayofweek')['consumptionliters'].mean()
monthly = df.groupby('month')['consumptionliters'].mean()

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(20, 5))
axes[0].bar(hourly.index, hourly.values, color='steelblue', edgecolor='white')
axes[0].set_title('Profil par heure de la journée'); axes[0].set_xlabel('Heure'); axes[0].set_ylabel('L / 15 min (moy.)')
axes[1].bar(range(7), weekday.values, color='teal', edgecolor='white')
axes[1].set_title('Profil hebdomadaire'); axes[1].set_xticks(range(7)); axes[1].set_xticklabels(day_labels)
axes[2].bar(range(len(monthly)), monthly.values, color='darkcyan', edgecolor='white')
axes[2].set_title('Profil mensuel'); axes[2].set_xticks(range(len(monthly)))
axes[2].set_xticklabels([month_labels.get(m, m) for m in monthly.index])
plt.suptitle('Patterns cycliques de consommation', fontweight='bold', y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig04_patterns_cycliques.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()
In [9]:
pivot = df.pivot_table(values='consumptionliters', index='hour', columns='dayofweek', aggfunc='mean')
pivot.columns = day_labels
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 7))
sns.heatmap(pivot, cmap='YlOrRd', linewidths=0.3, cbar_kws={'label':'L / 15 min (moy.)'}, ax=ax)
ax.set_title('Heatmap : consommation moyenne heure × jour', fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Jour'); ax.set_ylabel('Heure')
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig05_heatmap_heure_jour.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()

5. Analyse par zone et quartier¶

In [10]:
colors = {'residential':'#4E79A7','tourist':'#F28E2B','industrial':'#E15759'}
zstats = df.groupby('zone')['consumptionliters'].agg(['mean','median','std','sum']).sort_values('mean', ascending=False)
display(zstats.round(2))

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 5))
zc = [colors.get(z,'grey') for z in zstats.index]
axes[0].barh(zstats.index, zstats['mean'], color=zc, edgecolor='white')
axes[0].set_title('Consommation moyenne par zone (L / 15 min)')
for i,v in enumerate(zstats['mean']): axes[0].text(v+3, i, f'{v:.0f}', va='center')
axes[1].boxplot([df.loc[df.zone==z,'consumptionliters'] for z in zstats.index],
                labels=zstats.index, patch_artist=True, showfliers=False,
                medianprops=dict(color='navy', linewidth=2))
axes[1].set_title('Distribution par zone (sans extrêmes)')
plt.suptitle('Consommation par zone', fontweight='bold', y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig06_zone.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()
mean median std sum
zone
industrial 858.50 739.70 636.35 79452891.2
tourist 499.02 299.95 593.90 46183307.6
residential 262.85 227.20 246.65 24326114.3
In [11]:
hz = df.groupby(['hour','zone'])['consumptionliters'].mean().reset_index()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 5))
for z,c in colors.items():
    m = hz.zone==z
    ax.plot(hz.loc[m,'hour'], hz.loc[m,'consumptionliters'], label=z.capitalize(), color=c, lw=2.5, marker='o', ms=4)
ax.set_title('Profil journalier par zone', fontweight='bold'); ax.set_xlabel('Heure'); ax.set_ylabel('L / 15 min (moy.)')
ax.legend(title='Zone')
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig07_profil_zone.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()
In [12]:
if 'quartier' in df.columns:
    qs = df.groupby('quartier')['consumptionliters'].mean().sort_values(ascending=False)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 5))
    ax.bar(range(len(qs)), qs.values, color='steelblue', edgecolor='white', alpha=0.85)
    ax.set_xticks(range(len(qs))); ax.set_xticklabels(qs.index, rotation=45, ha='right', fontsize=9)
    ax.set_title('Consommation moyenne par quartier', fontweight='bold'); ax.set_ylabel('L / 15 min')
    plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig08_quartier.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()

6. Variables météo & corrélations¶

In [13]:
num = [c for c in ['consumptionliters','flowratelpm','pressurebar','temperaturec','rainfall',
                    'hour','dayofweek','month','household_size'] if c in df.columns]
corr = df[num].corr()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 8))
sns.heatmap(corr, mask=np.triu(np.ones_like(corr, bool)), cmap='RdBu_r', center=0,
            annot=True, fmt='.2f', square=True, linewidths=0.5, ax=ax)
ax.set_title('Matrice de corrélation', fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig09_correlation.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()

# Note : on VÉRIFIE la corrélation pluie/mois au lieu de la supposer
if 'rainfall' in df.columns:
    print(f"Corrélation rainfall ↔ month : {df['rainfall'].corr(df['month']):.2f}")
    print(f"Corrélation temperaturec ↔ consommation : {df['temperaturec'].corr(df['consumptionliters']):.2f}")

Corrélation rainfall ↔ month : -0.04
Corrélation temperaturec ↔ consommation : 0.37
In [14]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 5))
s = df.sample(min(5000, len(df)), random_state=42)
sc = axes[0].scatter(s['temperaturec'], s['consumptionliters'], c=s['hour'], cmap='plasma', alpha=0.4, s=8)
plt.colorbar(sc, ax=axes[0], label='Heure'); axes[0].set_title('Température vs consommation')
axes[0].set_xlabel('°C'); axes[0].set_ylabel('L / 15 min')
# bins de température calculés en local (PAS ajouté au df → évite la fuite de temp_bin)
tb = pd.cut(df['temperaturec'], bins=10)
tc = df.groupby(tb)['consumptionliters'].mean()
axes[1].bar(range(len(tc)), tc.values, color='darkorange', edgecolor='white')
axes[1].set_xticks(range(len(tc))); axes[1].set_xticklabels([f'{b.mid:.0f}°' for b in tc.index], rotation=45, fontsize=8)
axes[1].set_title('Conso moyenne par tranche de température')
plt.suptitle('Relation température — consommation', fontweight='bold', y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig10_temperature.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()

7. Événements spéciaux — Ramadan, Festival, Holiday¶

In [15]:
ev = df.groupby('event_type')['consumptionliters'].agg(['mean','median','count'])
display(ev.round(2))
# Effet chiffré (calculé, non supposé)
base = df.loc[df.event_type=='normal','consumptionliters'].mean()
for e in ev.index:
    if e != 'normal':
        eff = (ev.loc[e,'mean']/base - 1)*100
        print(f'  {e:<10} : {eff:+.1f}% vs jours normaux')

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
ax.bar(ev.index, ev['mean'], color=['#AEC6CF','#FFD700','#FF6B6B','#7DCEA0'][:len(ev)], edgecolor='white')
ax.set_title('Consommation moyenne par événement', fontweight='bold'); ax.set_ylabel('L / 15 min')
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig12_evenements.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()
mean median count
event_type
festival 1148.03 1151.50 3456
holiday 526.80 511.65 1152
normal 534.32 387.60 270732
ramadan 316.57 231.55 2304 
  festival   : +114.9% vs jours normaux
  holiday    : -1.4% vs jours normaux
  ramadan    : -40.8% vs jours normaux
In [16]:
if 'ramadan' in df['event_type'].values:
    rp = df[df.event_type=='ramadan'].groupby('hour')['consumptionliters'].mean()
    npf = df[df.event_type=='normal'].groupby('hour')['consumptionliters'].mean()
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 5))
    ax.plot(npf.index, npf.values, label='Normal', color='steelblue', lw=2.5, marker='o', ms=4)
    ax.plot(rp.index, rp.values, label='Ramadan', color='#DAA520', lw=2.5, marker='s', ms=4)
    ax.axvline(19, color='red', ls='--', alpha=0.5, label='Iftar (~19h)')
    ax.axvline(4,  color='purple', ls='--', alpha=0.5, label='Shour (~4h)')
    ax.set_title('Profil journalier : Ramadan vs normal', fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlabel('Heure'); ax.set_ylabel('L / 15 min'); ax.legend()
    plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig13_ramadan.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()
    # Vérifie la couverture du Ramadan (doit être ~30 jours)
    rj = df[df.event_type=='ramadan']['timestamp'].dt.date.nunique()
    print(f'⚠️  Jours marqués Ramadan : {rj} (attendu ≈ 29–30). Si trop faible → corriger le générateur.')

⚠️  Jours marqués Ramadan : 2 (attendu ≈ 29–30). Si trop faible → corriger le générateur.

8. Analyse des anomalies et fuites¶

In [17]:
lc = df['anomalylabel'].value_counts()
lp = df['anomalylabel'].value_counts(normalize=True)*100
for l in lc.index: print(f'  {l:<10} : {lc[l]:>7,} ({lp[l]:.2f}%)')

lcolors = {'normal':'#2ECC71','anomaly':'#E74C3C','leak':'#F39C12'}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 5))
axes[0].pie(lc.values, labels=lc.index, autopct='%1.2f%%',
            colors=[lcolors.get(l,'grey') for l in lc.index], startangle=90,
            wedgeprops=dict(edgecolor='white', linewidth=2))
axes[0].set_title('Répartition des labels')
axes[1].bar(lc.index, lc.values, color=[lcolors.get(l,'grey') for l in lc.index], edgecolor='white')
axes[1].set_yscale('log'); axes[1].set_title('Nombre de points (échelle log)')
plt.suptitle('Distribution anomalies / fuites', fontweight='bold', y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig14_anomalies.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()
print('\n⚖️  Déséquilibre ~4% → évaluer la détection en Précision/Rappel/F1 (PAS en accuracy).')

  normal     : 266,516 (95.99%)
  anomaly    :   6,972 (2.51%)
  leak       :   4,156 (1.50%)

⚖️  Déséquilibre ~4% → évaluer la détection en Précision/Rappel/F1 (PAS en accuracy).
In [18]:
feats = ['consumptionliters','flowratelpm','pressurebar']
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))
for ax,f in zip(axes, feats):
    for l,c in lcolors.items():
        if l in df['anomalylabel'].values:
            ax.hist(df.loc[df.anomalylabel==l, f].dropna(), bins=50, alpha=0.55,
                    color=c, label=l, density=True)
    ax.set_title(f); ax.legend()
plt.suptitle('Normal vs anomalie vs fuite — signatures', fontweight='bold', y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig15_signatures.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()

9. Décomposition saisonnière (série journalière, période = 7)¶

In [19]:
ds = df.groupby(df['timestamp'].dt.date)['consumptionliters'].sum()
ds.index = pd.to_datetime(ds.index); ds = ds.sort_index()
dec = seasonal_decompose(ds, model='additive', period=7)
fig, axes = plt.subplots(4, 1, figsize=(16, 11), sharex=True)
for ax,(d,t,c) in zip(axes, [(dec.observed,'Observée','steelblue'),(dec.trend,'Tendance','darkorange'),
                              (dec.seasonal,'Saisonnalité (hebdo)','green'),(dec.resid,'Résidus','red')]):
    ax.plot(d, color=c, lw=1.2); ax.set_ylabel(t)
axes[0].set_title('Décomposition — consommation journalière', fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig17_decomposition.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()

10. ACF / PACF — guide pour SARIMA¶

⚠️ Données 15 min → la saisonnalité journalière est au lag 96 (et hebdo au lag 672). On affiche donc 200 lags pour bien voir le pic journalier.

In [20]:
sample_meter = df['meterid'].unique()[0]
ts = df[df.meterid==sample_meter].set_index('timestamp')['consumptionliters']
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(16, 8))
plot_acf(ts,  lags=200, ax=axes[0], color='steelblue')
plot_pacf(ts, lags=200, ax=axes[1], color='darkorange', method='ywm')
axes[0].axvline(96, color='red', ls='--', alpha=0.6); axes[0].set_title('ACF (lag 96 = 1 jour)')
axes[1].axvline(96, color='red', ls='--', alpha=0.6); axes[1].set_title('PACF (lag 96 = 1 jour)')
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig18_acf_pacf.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()
In [21]:
adf = adfuller(ts.dropna(), autolag='AIC')
print(f'ADF stat : {adf[0]:.4f} | p-value : {adf[1]:.6f}')
print('✅ Stationnaire (p<0.05)' if adf[1]<0.05 else '⚠️ Non stationnaire → différenciation')

ADF stat : -11.8770 | p-value : 0.000000
✅ Stationnaire (p<0.05)

11. Carte géographique des compteurs¶

In [22]:
if {'latitude','longitude'}.issubset(df.columns):
    geo = df.groupby('meterid').agg(latitude=('latitude','first'), longitude=('longitude','first'),
            zone=('zone','first'), mean_conso=('consumptionliters','mean'),
            anomaly_rate=('anomalylabel', lambda x:(x!='normal').mean()*100)).reset_index()
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 8))
    for z,c in colors.items():
        m = geo.zone==z
        ax.scatter(geo.loc[m,'longitude'], geo.loc[m,'latitude'], s=geo.loc[m,'mean_conso'],
                   c=c, alpha=0.7, edgecolors='k', label=z.capitalize())
    for _,r in geo.iterrows():
        ax.annotate(r['meterid'], (r['longitude'], r['latitude']), fontsize=7, ha='center')
    ax.set_title('Compteurs — Grand Agadir (taille = conso moyenne)', fontweight='bold')
    ax.set_xlabel('Longitude'); ax.set_ylabel('Latitude'); ax.legend()
    plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig19_carte.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()

12. Split temporel & sauvegarde (sans temp_bin)¶

In [23]:
# Exclure uniquement le dernier jour s'il est incomplet
last_day = df['timestamp'].dt.date.max()
cnt_last = (df['timestamp'].dt.date == last_day).sum()
if cnt_last < df.meterid.nunique() * STEPS_PER_DAY * 0.9:
    df = df[df['timestamp'].dt.date < last_day].copy()
    print(f'ℹ️  Dernier jour ({last_day}) incomplet → exclu.')

SPLIT_DATE = pd.Timestamp('2025-08-15')
df_train = df[df.timestamp <  SPLIT_DATE].copy()
df_test  = df[df.timestamp >= SPLIT_DATE].copy()

DATA_DIR = Path('../data'); DATA_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
df_train.to_csv(DATA_DIR/'train.csv', index=False)
df_test.to_csv(DATA_DIR/'test.csv',  index=False)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 5))
dt = df_train.groupby(df_train.timestamp.dt.date)['consumptionliters'].sum()
dv = df_test.groupby(df_test.timestamp.dt.date)['consumptionliters'].sum()
dt.index = pd.to_datetime(dt.index); dv.index = pd.to_datetime(dv.index)
ax.fill_between(dt.index, dt.values/1000, alpha=0.5, color='steelblue', label='Train')
ax.fill_between(dv.index, dv.values/1000, alpha=0.5, color='darkorange', label='Test')
ax.axvline(SPLIT_DATE, color='red', ls='--', lw=2, label=f'Coupure {SPLIT_DATE.date()}')
ax.set_title('Répartition train / test', fontweight='bold'); ax.set_ylabel('Milliers L / jour'); ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout(); plt.savefig(FIG_DIR/'fig20_split.png', bbox_inches='tight'); plt.show()

print(f'Train : {len(df_train):,} ({len(df_train)/len(df)*100:.1f}%) | Test : {len(df_test):,} ({len(df_test)/len(df)*100:.1f}%)')
print('Colonnes sauvegardées :', list(df_train.columns))
assert 'temp_bin' not in df_train.columns, 'temp_bin ne doit pas être sauvegardé'

ℹ️  Dernier jour (2025-09-30) incomplet → exclu.

Train : 224,640 (80.9%) | Test : 52,992 (19.1%)
Colonnes sauvegardées : ['timestamp', 'meterid', 'zone', 'quartier', 'latitude', 'longitude', 'consumptionliters', 'flowratelpm', 'pressurebar', 'temperaturec', 'rainfall', 'hour', 'dayofweek', 'month', 'isweekend', 'season', 'household_size', 'event_type', 'anomalylabel']

13. Synthèse (chiffres calculés, non supposés)¶

In [24]:
summer = df[df.month.isin([6,7,8,9])]['consumptionliters'].mean()
other  = df[~df.month.isin([6,7,8,9])]['consumptionliters'].mean()
print('SYNTHÈSE EDA — INSIGHTS POUR LA MODÉLISATION')
print('-'*55)
print(f'Pas de temps        : 15 min ({STEPS_PER_DAY} pts/jour) → saisonnalité SARIMA = 96')
print(f'Effet estival       : {(summer/other-1)*100:+.1f}% (été vs reste)')
print(f'Corr. température    : {df["temperaturec"].corr(df["consumptionliters"]):.2f}')
print(f'Labels              : ' + ' | '.join(f'{l} {df.anomalylabel.eq(l).mean()*100:.1f}%' for l in df.anomalylabel.unique()))
print('Prévision  → SARIMA[96] / Prophet / XGBoost / LSTM (notebook 02)')
print('Détection  → Isolation Forest / LSTM-AE / 3-sigma STL (notebook 03)')
print('\n🏁 EDA terminée → 02_prediction.ipynb')

SYNTHÈSE EDA — INSIGHTS POUR LA MODÉLISATION
-------------------------------------------------------
Pas de temps        : 15 min (96 pts/jour) → saisonnalité SARIMA = 96
Effet estival       : +112.8% (été vs reste)
Corr. température    : 0.37
Labels              : normal 96.0% | anomaly 2.5% | leak 1.5%
Prévision  → SARIMA[96] / Prophet / XGBoost / LSTM (notebook 02)
Détection  → Isolation Forest / LSTM-AE / 3-sigma STL (notebook 03)
